sections.question: I am a part time city Attorney for the city of Tracy. I also have a general law practice. I have been named as the Executor of the estate of the individual who owned and operated the cab company within the Tracy area, including one [serving] City of Tracy. I have no beneficial interest in the estate. The city desires to acquire the cab business, including all of its assets. I would execute the Bill of sale after court approval. The City Attorney acts as the legal adviser to the Council and City Staffs. I do not have any vote on any of the issues which are decided by the Council. If the city acquires the cab company it will be necessary for the city Council to approve the purchase. Under these circumstances, in your opinion, do I have a sufficient interest in the property or substantial indirect participation in the actions of the Council as to constitute a conflict of interest under the Fair Political Practices Act.
sections.conclusion: It is the opinion of the staff that your participation in the city's purchase of the cab company would be a conflict of interest. Even though the city council must ultimately approve the purchase and you have no vote on the matter, your rendition of legal advice regarding the purchase or review of the purchase contract, as City Attorney, brings your participation within the purview of this regulation. Thus, since you participate in the making of the decision within the meaning of the Act, you must disqualify yourself from the decision-making process if you have a financial interest in the decision. According to information supplied by you, the sale of this cab company would materially effect the estate and would generate executor's fees amounting to $700-900. Thus, a decision by the city council to purchase the company will affect a source of income to you in a financially material way. You have a financial interest in the decision within the meaning of Government code §87100 and §7103.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city attorney who is also the executor of an estate containing a cab company, and who would provide legal advice regarding the city council's potential purchase of that cab company, has a sufficient financial interest or participatory role to constitute a conflict of interest under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that the requester has a conflict of interest and must disqualify himself from participating in the city council's decision to purchase the cab company because: (1) his legal advice to the city on the purchase constitutes participation in the decision-making process, and (2) he has a material financial interest in the outcome through executor's fees of $700-900. The execution of the bill of sale after court approval in his capacity as executor is not itself a governmental decision and presents no conflict.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (July 29, 1975) and FPPC response (August 26, 1976). OCR quality is moderate with some character recognition errors but content is reasonably clear. The question and conclusion sections are clearly identifiable and extracted verbatim from the FPPC response letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: I am a part time city Attorney for the city of Tracy. I also have a general law practice. I have been named as the Executor of the estate of the individual who owned and operated the cab company within the Tracy area, including one [serving] City of Tracy. I have no beneficial interest in the estate. The city desires to acquire the cab business, including all of its assets. I would execute the Bill of sale after court approval. The City Attorney acts as the legal adviser to the Council and City Staffs. I do not have any vote on any of the issues which are decided by the Council. If the city acquires the cab company it will be necessary for the city Council to approve the purchase. Under these circumstances, in your opinion, do I have a sufficient interest in the property or substantial indirect participation in the actions of the Council as to constitute a conflict of interest under the Fair Political Practices Act.

CONCLUSION: It is the opinion of the staff that your participation in the city's purchase of the cab company would be a conflict of interest. Even though the city council must ultimately approve the purchase and you have no vote on the matter, your rendition of legal advice regarding the purchase or review of the purchase contract, as City Attorney, brings your participation within the purview of this regulation. Thus, since you participate in the making of the decision within the meaning of the Act, you must disqualify yourself from the decision-making process if you have a financial interest in the decision. According to information supplied by you, the sale of this cab company would materially effect the estate and would generate executor's fees amounting to $700-900. Thus, a decision by the city council to purchase the company will affect a source of income to you in a financially material way. You have a financial interest in the decision within the meaning of Government code §87100 and §7103.

embedding.summary: FPPC advice letter responding to a city attorney's inquiry about whether serving as both legal adviser to the city council on a potential purchase of a cab company and as executor of an estate containing that company creates a conflict of interest. The FPPC concludes that a conflict exists and the city attorney must disqualify himself from advising on the purchase decision.
